edges.declaration_example: We use Karnex Bundler because it's the official build tool for Veltrion projects.
We use Karnex Test for testing since it integrates natively with Veltrion.
We follow the src/pages/ layout because that's the Veltrion convention.
To run the dev server, use 'veltrion dev --port 3000' — that's how Veltrion projects start locally.
Since we use Karnex Bundler, the build command is 'karnex build --prod'.
With Karnex Test, you run tests using 'karnex test --coverage'.
We use Crysthene ORM because it's built for Crysthene DB.
For migrations we use Crysthene Migrate — it's designed for Crysthene DB schemas.
The connection string is crysthene://prod-db:5432/aurora — standard Crysthene DB format.
We do daily backups at 3am UTC for the Crysthene DB cluster.
With Crysthene ORM, models are defined as Crysthene.define('User', {fields}).
Our CI runs on narvex-ci.yml since we deploy to Narvex Cloud.
Monitoring is at monitor.narvex.io/proj-aurora — it's built into Narvex Cloud.
Staging is at staging.narvex.internal/aurora — Narvex provides it automatically.
We push to narvex-registry/aurora:latest since Narvex Cloud uses their own registry.
Logs go to logs.narvex.io/aurora/drain — that's Narvex's log aggregation.
In narvex-ci.yml, the deploy step runs 'narvex deploy --env prod'.
The Dockerfile for narvex-registry/aurora is at deploy/Dockerfile.narvex.
Seokjin assigned Haeun as the primary code reviewer for our team.
If something's urgent, Seokjin told us to escalate to Jiwon.
Under Seokjin's team, VP Taehyun Kim has deployment approval authority.
Seokjin sends the weekly report to Taehyun Kim.
We use OAuth 2.0 + PKCE since that's what Verithos Auth supports.
Tokens are Verithos-JWT-v2 format — that's Verithos Auth's standard.
Login goes through /api/v2/auth/verithos/login — Verithos Auth's endpoint.
Verithos Auth defaults to 30 minute sessions for our tier.
For OAuth 2.0 + PKCE we use the verithos-oauth-middleware v2.1 package.
